definition: The host vessel/vehicle/node (USV, landing craft, barge, watercraft hull) that carries the sensors, effectors and C2; the thing being floated and moved.
maritime_note: Army watercraft hulls: MSV(L) new-construction; LCU/LSV/MCS under ESP/SLEP; candidate uncrewed surface vessels.

definition: Radar, EO/IR, sonar and ISR payloads that detect and track — the find/fix/track inputs to the chain.
maritime_note: Hydrographic & surf-zone survey, maritime-domain ISR/screening; autonomy perception often files under non-maritime PSC/NAICS.

definition: Weapons / kinetic means of action that engage a threat. Non-kinetic cargo, bridging, rafting and survey payloads are explicitly NOT effectors.
maritime_note: Expected BLANK for Army maritime today (logistics / operational-maneuver mission, not strike). A blank node is a valid, expected answer — do not back-fill it with cargo/bridging to avoid emptiness.

definition: Battle-management integration that fuses sensor data, decides, and tasks the effector. In this 4-node collapse it absorbs autonomy, assured PNT, comms/networking and systems integration.
maritime_note: MIBS bridge integration; Harbormaster C2 Center (HCCC); autonomous-operations enablement; assured PNT; resilient / BLOS comms.
